mnemonics: Connected Like a Country: A metric space is connected if it cannot be split into two separate, non-overlapping open sets (like two countries with a border between them). If there's a gap, it's disconnected.
keyPoints: A metric space is connected if it is not the union of two nonempty disjoint open sets.
Continuous images of connected sets are connected.
The only subsets of a metric space that are both open and closed are the empty set and the entire space itself.
commonMistakes: Confusing path-connectedness with connectedness (all path-connected spaces are connected, but not all connected spaces are path-connected).
Assuming that if a set is not open, it must be closed, and vice-versa, in the context of connectedness. A set can be neither.
